section.title: Description
section.p: The ingestion engine converts Markdown-based portfolio content into structured artifacts used by the retrieval system.
This process runs offline as a Python-based pipeline and is responsible for preparing all data required for runtime retrieval.
The ingestion process is divided into two distinct phases:
This separation ensures a clean boundary between data preparation and vector indexing.

section.title: Implementation Language
section.p: The ingestion pipeline is implemented as a set of Python scripts to align with:

section.title: Responsibilities
section.p: The ingestion engine performs the following operations:

ol.li: Generate content chunks using document structure
Load chunk data from JSON artifacts
Generate embeddings using a sentence-transformer model

section.title: Chunking Strategy
section.p: Documents are chunked using heading-based segmentation.
Each chunk represents a semantically meaningful section of a document.
This structure enables downstream document-aware retrieval.

section.title: Output Artifacts
section.p: The ingestion pipeline produces the following files:
chunks.json contains all text chunks and associated metadata.
knowledge_graph.json stores document relationships as an adjacency map.
kb_index.faiss contains vector embeddings for similarity search.

section.title: Execution
section.p: The ingestion and indexing pipeline is executed via:
